title: Single-Stage Diffusion NeRF: A Unified Approach to 3D Generation and Reconstruction
problem_statement: Developing a comprehensive model for 3D-aware image synthesis that handles both scene generation and novel view synthesis from images remains challenging due to the need for task-specific methods. Existing approaches rely on two-stage training with pretrained NeRFs as real data, which limits their flexibility and efficiency.

core_ideas.name: Single-Stage Diffusion NeRF (SSDNeRF)
core_ideas.mechanism: A unified approach that employs an expressive diffusion model to learn a generalizable prior of neural radiance fields (NeRF) from multi-view images, using a new single-stage training paradigm with an end-to-end objective that jointly optimizes a NeRF auto-decoder and a latent diffusion model.
target_limitations: lack of unified model for generation and reconstruction
evidence_phrases: single-stage training paradigm with an end-to-end objective that jointly optimizes a NeRF auto-decoder and a latent diffusion model

evidence_refs.quote: single-stage training paradigm with an end-to-end objective that jointly optimizes a NeRF auto-decoder and a latent diffusion model

experiment_signals.setting: unconditional generation and single/sparse-view 3D reconstruction
experiment_signals.claim: SSDNeRF demonstrates robust results comparable to or better than leading task-specific methods in unconditional generation and single/sparse-view 3D reconstruction.
evidence_phrases: SSDNeRF demonstrates robust results comparable to or better than leading task-specific methods in unconditional generation and single/sparse-view 3D reconstruction

evidence_refs.quote: SSDNeRF demonstrates robust results comparable to or better than leading task-specific methods in unconditional generation and single/sparse-view 3D reconstruction

title: PhysCtrl: Generative Physics for Controllable and Physics-Grounded Video Generation
problem_statement: Existing video generation models produce photo-realistic videos but often lack physical plausibility and 3D controllability. PhysCtrl addresses this by enabling physics-grounded image-to-video generation with explicit physical parameters and force control.

core_ideas.name: Generative Physics Network
core_ideas.mechanism: A diffusion model conditioned on physics parameters and applied forces learns the distribution of physical dynamics across four materials (elastic, sand, plasticine, and rigid), representing dynamics as 3D point trajectories trained on a large-scale synthetic dataset.
evidence_phrases: generative physics network that learns the distribution of physical dynamics across four materials (elastic, sand, plasticine, and rigid) via a diffusion model conditioned on physics parameters and applied forces
represent physical dynamics as 3D point trajectories and train on a large-scale synthetic dataset of 550K animations generated by physics simulators

evidence_refs.quote: generative physics network that learns the distribution of physical dynamics across four materials (elastic, sand, plasticine, and rigid) via a diffusion model conditioned on physics parameters and applied forces

core_ideas.name: Spatiotemporal Attention with Physics Constraints
core_ideas.mechanism: The diffusion model is enhanced with a novel spatiotemporal attention block that emulates particle interactions and incorporates physics-based constraints during training to enforce physical plausibility.
evidence_phrases: enhance the diffusion model with a novel spatiotemporal attention block that emulates particle interactions and incorporates physics-based constraints during training to enforce physical plausibility

evidence_refs.quote: enhance the diffusion model with a novel spatiotemporal attention block that emulates particle interactions and incorporates physics-based constraints during training to enforce physical plausibility

experiment_signals.setting: Physics-grounded image-to-video generation
experiment_signals.claim: PhysCtrl generates realistic, physics-grounded motion trajectories that, when used to drive image-to-video models, yield high-fidelity, controllable videos that outperform existing methods in both visual quality and physical plausibility.
evidence_phrases: Experiments show that PhysCtrl generates realistic, physics-grounded motion trajectories which, when used to drive image-to-video models, yield high-fidelity, controllable videos that outperform existing methods in both visual quality and physical plausibility

evidence_refs.quote: Experiments show that PhysCtrl generates realistic, physics-grounded motion trajectories which, when used to drive image-to-video models, yield high-fidelity, controllable videos that outperform existing methods in both visual quality and physical plausibility

problem_statement: The core technical problem is creating 4D (dynamic 3D) scenes from monocular video. This requires synthesizing novel views at arbitrary camera poses and timestamps, and enabling robust 4D reconstruction from a single video input.

title: Gen3C: 3D-Informed World-Consistent Video Generation with Precise Camera Control
problem_statement: Prior video generation models produce realistic videos but leverage little 3D information, leading to temporal inconsistencies like objects popping in and out. Additionally, camera control is imprecise because camera parameters are mere inputs that the network must interpret, rather than being explicitly guided. This results in a lack of 3D consistency and precise camera trajectory following.

core_ideas.name: 3D Cache Guidance
core_ideas.mechanism: Gen3C is guided by a 3D cache consisting of point clouds obtained by predicting pixel-wise depth of seed images or previously generated frames. This 3D structure is used to condition the generation process.
evidence_phrases: Gen3C is guided by a 3D cache: point clouds obtained by predicting the pixel-wise depth of seed images or previously generated frames.

evidence_refs.quote: Gen3C is guided by a 3D cache: point clouds obtained by predicting the pixel-wise depth of seed images or previously generated frames.

core_ideas.name: Camera-Conditioned Rendering
core_ideas.mechanism: When generating the next frames, Gen3C is conditioned on the 2D renderings of the 3D cache with the new camera trajectory provided by the user, enabling precise camera control without the network inferring structure from pose.
evidence_phrases: When generating the next frames, Gen3C is conditioned on the 2D renderings of the 3D cache with the new camera trajectory provided by the user.

evidence_refs.quote: When generating the next frames, Gen3C is conditioned on the 2D renderings of the 3D cache with the new camera trajectory provided by the user.

core_ideas.name: Focused Generative Power
core_ideas.mechanism: By conditioning on the 3D cache renderings, the model focuses generative power on previously unobserved regions and advancing the scene state, rather than remembering past frames or inferring image structure from camera pose.
evidence_phrases: The model, instead, can focus all its generative power on previously unobserved regions, as well as advancing the scene state to the next frame.

evidence_refs.quote: The model, instead, can focus all its generative power on previously unobserved regions, as well as advancing the scene state to the next frame.

experiment_signals.setting: sparse-view novel view synthesis
experiment_signals.claim: Gen3C achieves state-of-the-art results in sparse-view novel view synthesis, even in challenging settings such as driving scenes and monocular dynamic video.
evidence_phrases: Our results demonstrate more precise camera control than prior work, as well as state-of-the-art results in sparse-view novel view synthesis, even in challenging settings such as driving scenes and monocular dynamic video.

evidence_refs.quote: Our results demonstrate more precise camera control than prior work, as well as state-of-the-art results in sparse-view novel view synthesis, even in challenging settings such as driving scenes and monocular dynamic video.

title: SV4D: Dynamic 3D Content Generation with Multi-Frame and Multi-View Consistency
problem_statement: Generating dynamic 3D content from a monocular video requires multi-frame and multi-view consistency. Previous methods rely on separate generative models for video generation and novel view synthesis, which can be cumbersome and lack unified consistency. SV4D addresses this by designing a unified diffusion model to generate novel view videos of dynamic 3D objects.

core_ideas.name: Unified Latent Video Diffusion Model
core_ideas.mechanism: A single latent video diffusion model generates novel view videos for each frame of a monocular reference video, ensuring temporal consistency, which are then used to optimize an implicit 4D representation (dynamic NeRF).
target_limitations: Separate generative models for video and view synthesis

core_ideas.name: Dynamic 3D Object Dataset Curation

target_limitations: Lack of training data for dynamic 3D objects

evidence_refs.quote: To train our unified novel view video generation model, we curate a dynamic 3D object dataset from the existing Objaverse dataset

Bridging generation and novel view synthesis remains a major challenge. The core technical problem is achieving unified 4D scene generation with both temporal continuity and geometric consistency.

core_ideas.name: PhiGenesis unified framework

title: Warm Diffusion: Recipe for Blur-Noise Mixture Diffusion Models
problem_statement: Diffusion models face a trade-off between hot diffusion (noise-only) which fails to exploit high-frequency/low-frequency correlations and exhibits random early-step behavior, and cold diffusion (blur-only) which neglects noise's role in shaping the data manifold, causing out-of-manifold issues. The core technical problem is how to jointly control blurring and noise to integrate their respective strengths while avoiding their individual weaknesses.

title: Residual Denoising Diffusion Models
problem_statement: Traditional single denoising diffusion models are uninterpretable for image restoration. RDDM decouples the diffusion process into residual and noise components to unify image generation and restoration. UniReal addresses this by unifying these tasks under a video generation paradigm, treating image tasks as discontinuous video generation to learn real-world dynamics.

core_ideas.name: Discontinuous Video Generation for Image Tasks
core_ideas.mechanism: Treats varying numbers of input and output images as frames in a video, enabling a unified model for generation, editing, and composition by learning from large-scale videos.

core_ideas.name: Learning Real-world Dynamics from Videos
core_ideas.mechanism: Leverages videos as a scalable source for universal supervision, allowing the model to learn complex dynamics like shadows, reflections, and object interaction.
evidence_phrases: leverage videos as a scalable source for universal supervision

evidence_refs.quote: leverage videos as a scalable source for universal supervision

title: Lavida-O: Elastic Large Masked Diffusion Models for Unified Multimodal Understanding and Generation
problem_statement: Existing multimodal Masked Diffusion Models (MDMs) such as MMaDa and Muddit are limited to simple image-level understanding tasks and low-resolution image generation. This restricts their applicability for unified multimodal understanding and generation at high resolutions.

core_ideas.name: Elastic Mixture-of-Transformers (Elastic-MoT) architecture

title: Does FLUX Already Know How to Perform Physically Plausible Image Composition?

title: 4D-fy: Text-to-4D Generation Using Hybrid Score Distillation Sampling
problem_statement: Current text-to-4D generation methods face a three-way tradeoff between the quality of scene appearance, 3D structure, and motion. Text-to-image models produce realistic appearance and 3D structure but no motion, while text-to-video models produce motion but poorer appearance and 3D structure. These complementary strengths and opposing weaknesses make it difficult to combine them to alleviate this tradeoff.

title: TiViBench: Benchmarking Think-in-Video Reasoning for Video Generative Models
problem_statement: Existing benchmarks for video generative models predominantly evaluate visual fidelity and temporal coherence, failing to capture higher-order reasoning abilities similar to large language models. This gap makes it unclear whether models can exhibit reasoning capabilities beyond producing visually plausible outputs. The core problem is the lack of systematic evaluation for physical plausibility and logical consistency in video generation.

title: UniVideo: Unified Understanding, Generation, and Editing for Videos
problem_statement: Unified multimodal models have been largely limited to the image domain, lacking a versatile framework for video generation and editing under a single multimodal instruction paradigm. This paper addresses the challenge of extending unified modeling to the video domain while preserving text generation capabilities and enabling accurate interpretation of complex multimodal instructions.

core_ideas.name: Dual-stream architecture
core_ideas.mechanism: Combines a Multimodal Large Language Model (MLLM) for instruction understanding with a Multimodal DiT (MMDiT) for video generation, preserving MLLM's text generation capabilities and enabling accurate interpretation of complex multimodal instructions.
evidence_phrases: UniVideo adopts a dual-stream design, combining a Multimodal Large Language Model (MLLM) for instruction understanding with a Multimodal DiT (MMDiT) for video generation

evidence_refs.quote: UniVideo adopts a dual-stream design, combining a Multimodal Large Language Model (MLLM) for instruction understanding with a Multimodal DiT (MMDiT) for video generation
